channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: "Pink Planet" is surrounded by salty clouds, researchers using Webb telescope find - CBS News
item.description:
1. "Pink Planet" is surrounded by salty clouds, researchers using Webb telescope find  CBS News
  2. Famous ‘Pink Planet’ harbors a salty surprise  Northwestern Now News
  3. The Pink Planet is so weird, astronomers struggle to define it. And they've just found it's covered in salty clouds  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. Webb telescope finds possible salt clouds on a pink alien world  Mashable
  5. "Pink Planet" GJ504b Could Be First World With Clouds Made Of Salt, JWST Data Suggests  IFLScience

item.title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it. - Space
item.description:
1. 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.  Space
  2. A bold satellite rescue mission came together in record time, but will it work?  Ars Technica
  3. Swift reboost mission ready for launch  SpaceNews
  4. Rocket Attached to Aircraft for Katalyst-NASA Swift Boost  NASA Science (.gov)
  5. A space telescope is falling to Earth. NASA plans a mission to rescue it  USA Today

item.description:

item.title: Some tropical butterfly species have unlocked an evolutionary way to live longer - CNN
item.description:
1. Some tropical butterfly species have unlocked an evolutionary way to live longer  CNN

  3. The Butterfly Longevity Diet  The Atlantic
  4. Butterflies That Eat Pollen May Hold Clues to Extreme Longevity  ScienceAlert
  5. 'Geriatric' butterfly species lives nearly three times as long as their relatives  EurekAlert!

item.title: 'A completely different story': 300 million-year-old fossils reveal the first vertebrate land dwellers weren't what we thought, researchers claim - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'A completely different story': 300 million-year-old fossils reveal the first vertebrate land dwellers weren't what we thought, researchers claim  Live Science
  2. Baby crocodile-like fossils just blew up a long-held evolution theory  Popular Science
  3. Earth’s First Land Animals May Never Have Been Amphibian-Like After All  Sci.News
  4. A textbook assumption about early land vertebrates may be wrong  Science News
  5. Fossilized babies of ancient crocodile-like predators uproot understanding of how animals adapted to the land  Phys.org

item.title: The Sun may not engulf Earth after all, scientists say - Phys.org
item.description:
1. The Sun may not engulf Earth after all, scientists say  Phys.org
  2. Sun may spare Earth and Mars, as study reverses course on death of our planet  WION
  3. Complex life on Earth may last 500 million years longer than expected  New Scientist
  4. The real reason Earth escapes the dying sun gravity  The News International
  5. Earth might actually survive sun’s fiery death as new study overturns decades-old forecast  Interesting Engineering

item.title: It's Official: Voyager 1 Will Break A Cosmic Record, Reaching 1 Light-Day From Earth On November 18, 2026 - IFLScience

item.title: Scientists Spent 13 Years Bouncing Radar Off Europa. Here’s What They Found - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Scientists Spent 13 Years Bouncing Radar Off Europa. Here’s What They Found  Gizmodo
  2. Could Earth have sent life to Jupiter's moon Europa?  Phys.org
  3. Hundreds of Thousands of Quadrillions of Bacteria Could Have Reached a Moon of Jupiter from Earth in the Last 80 Million Years  La Brújula Verde
  4. Europa’s Ice Shell Gets Its Best Radar Portrait Yet: A 13-Year Dataset Arrives  Tech Times
  5. Europa, Jupiter's icy moon, intrigues scientists after radar indicates an unusual brightness in the ice and raises new suspicions about what might be hidden in the deep layers of this distant world.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: Consciousness Could Exist in Bodies Nothing Like Ours, Researchers Say - ScienceAlert

item.title: In 1908, something exploded above the remote Siberian forest of Tunguska with enough force to flatten roughly 2,000 square kilometres of trees — and more than a century later, no impact crater has ever been found, because the object, probably a small aster - Space Daily

item.title: Denisovan DNA influences the immune systems of modern Oceanians — but researchers aren't sure why - Live Science
item.description:

  4. Denisovan DNA still switches genes on and off in humans  Earth.com

item.title: The American flags planted by Apollo astronauts have likely bleached pure white after 50 years of unfiltered solar radiation, leaving the most famous banners in history standing as blank cloth - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The American flags planted by Apollo astronauts have likely bleached pure white after 50 years of unfiltered solar radiation, leaving the most famous banners in history standing as blank cloth  Space Daily
  2. Apollo 11's flag was knocked flat by the lunar module's own exhaust the instant the astronauts lifted off, meaning the first banner on the moon stood upright for less than a day  Space Daily
  3. Apollo astronauts planted American flags on the Moon- 50 years of unfiltered sunlight may have bleached them into blank white cloth  The Times of India

  3. ESA rover Rosalind Franklin, designed to look for molecules of life on Mars, passes a stress test  starlust.org
  4. Seven organic molecules never seen before on Mars just turned up inside a single rock — and one is a precursor to DNA  ecoportal.net
  5. ExoMars: Stress Test For Search For Life  Mirage News

item.title: Moon Impact 3.5 Billion Years Ago Hints at Hidden Era of Solar System - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Moon Impact 3.5 Billion Years Ago Hints at Hidden Era of Solar System  ScienceAlert
  2. A lunar meteorite found in northwest Africa has been shown to record evidence of an asteroid impact on the Moon 3.5 billion years ago, matching independently dated impacts on Earth and on the asteroid 4 Vesta from the same period, at the moment life on Ea  Space Daily
  3. Giant, 3.5 billion-year-old impact turned the moon's surface molten, rare lunar meteorite reveals  Live Science
  4. A meteorite sat in an African desert for years, and scientists have only just discovered what it contained  MSN
  5. An ancient piece of the moon was found in Africa, and it may have melted part of the Moon’s surface while Earth’s earliest life was beginning to emerge  The Times of India
